format.expectation: API contracts, DB schemas, technology choices, deployment, failure handling

problems.name: Card Delivery System
problems.context: Physical card manufacturing and delivery to customers

api.create_card: POST /cards { user_id, card_type, shipping_address } -> { card_id, estimated_delivery }

problems.schema:
cards(id, user_id, card_type, status, created_at)
deliveries(id, card_id, courier_id, tracking_number, status, shipping_address)
delivery_events(id, delivery_id, event_type, location, timestamp, raw_payload)
failure_handling: Courier API down -> queue and retry with backoff
Duplicate webhooks -> idempotency key on (tracking_number, event_type, timestamp)
Lost card -> trigger reissue, notify user

problems.name: Payment Notification Service
problems.context: Notify users of payment events at scale
problems.idempotency_flow:
1. Receive PaymentEvent with event_id
2. Check if notification with event_id exists
3. If exists -> skip
4. If not -> create notification, send to channel
failure_handling: Provider timeout -> retry with exponential backoff (max 3)
Provider down -> fallback to secondary or queue
No push token -> fallback to SMS/email

problems.name: Distributed Rate Limiter
problems.context: Protect APIs from abuse, per-user and global limits
failure_handling: Redis down -> fail open (allow) or fail closed (reject)
Clock skew -> use Redis server time
Thundering herd -> Lua script for atomic check-and-increment

problems.name: Apartment Booking System
problems.context: Book apartments via 3rd party provider API
problems.state_machine:
SEARCHING -> SELECTED -> RESERVED -> CONFIRMED -> COMPLETED
                     |           |
                  EXPIRED     CANCELLED
problems.double_booking_prevention:
Option 1: Unique constraint on (apartment_id, dates) WHERE status IN ('RESERVED', 'CONFIRMED')
Option 2: SELECT FOR UPDATE with daterange overlap check
failure_handling: 3rd party timeout -> retry once, then fail gracefully
Payment fails after reservation -> release reservation
Reservation expires -> background job cancels and notifies

problems.name: Temporary Debit Card Issuance
problems.context: Issue virtual cards with expiry and spending limits
problems.authorization_flow:
1. Receive auth request
2. Lookup card by card_number_hash
3. Check: status = 'ACTIVE' AND expires_at > NOW()
4. Check: spent_amount + amount <= spending_limit
5. If pass -> approve, increment spent_amount atomically
6. Log transaction
problems.atomic_spend_update: UPDATE temporary_cards SET spent_amount = spent_amount + ? WHERE id = ? AND status = 'ACTIVE' AND expires_at > NOW() AND spent_amount + ? <= spending_limit RETURNING id;

common_followups: What happens if payment succeeds but notification fails?
What if the 3rd party API is down for 2 hours?
User clicks pay twice — what happens?
Two users book the same slot at the same time?
What indexes do you need? Why?
How do you deploy without downtime?
How do you know if this system is healthy?
